gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: Reentrancy in claim/delegateClaim via unguarded delegatecall to PORTAL_ROLLER
candidate.claimed_root_cause: No reentrancy guard on claim/delegateClaim; PORTAL_ROLLER may transfer before zeroing claimable balance (CEI violation)
candidate.claimed_impact: Attacker (beneficiary) double-claims LP fees, draining BNB/quote token beyond entitlement

live_preconditions.name: Token with non-zero claimable LP fees exists (lpLocks populated post-DEX migration)
live_preconditions.observed_value: not present in live_context.json; listed under missing_until_acte_v3_refresh
live_preconditions.command_if_missing: cast call 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0 'getLocks(address)' <token> --rpc-url $BSC_RPC on a token address derived from recent LaunchedToDEX events

live_preconditions.name: Attacker is registered beneficiary of that token (_tokenBeneficiaries[token] == attacker)
live_preconditions.observed_value: not present in live_context.json; mapping keys not enumerable without event-derived keys
live_preconditions.command_if_missing: decode BeneficiaryClaimed / setTokenBeneficiary events from BSC logs for the proxy address to find live beneficiary-token pairs

live_preconditions.name: PORTAL_ROLLER implementation source available for static CEI analysis
live_preconditions.observed_value: No PortalRoller.sol present in src/; only out/IPortal.sol/IRoller.json (ABI artifact) found; PORTAL_ROLLER is an immutable address set at construction with no indexed source
live_preconditions.command_if_missing: Read PORTAL_ROLLER address from Portal constructor args or storage, then fetch verified source from BscScan: cast storage 0xeb6c62d1885c421797d0c494e693c90d4b54125a <PORTAL_ROLLER_slot> --rpc-url $BSC_RPC; then curl https://api.bscscan.com/api?module=contract&action=getsourcecode&address=<PORTAL_ROLLER_addr>

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Sole modifier is onlyIfBitFlagsSet (bitflag read, no reentrancy protection); immediately delegates to PORTAL_ROLLER with no guard
Identical pattern to claim; same absence of reentrancy guard
Raw assembly delegatecall; any reentrancy protection must live entirely inside PORTAL_ROLLER; Portal itself adds none
PORTAL_ROLLER is immutable, set at construction; its source is not present in the indexed repository, making static CEI verification impossible
Only modifier on claim/delegateClaim; confirmed to be a simple bitflag read with no reentrancy semantics

attacker_path.actor: Unprivileged attacker who is the registered beneficiary of a launched token with non-zero claimable LP fees
attacker_inputs: token address (the token for which attacker is beneficiary)
attacker-deployed contract with receive() that re-enters Portal.claim(token)
call_sequence: 3. PORTAL_ROLLER (running in Portal storage context) reads claimable LP fee amount
4. IF PORTAL_ROLLER transfers BNB/quote token before zeroing claimable balance: attacker receive() fires
6. PORTAL_ROLLER reads same non-zeroed claimable amount, transfers again
7. Attacker drains more than single-claim entitlement
state_before: lpLocks[token] has non-zero claimable LP fee amount
state_after: IF vulnerable: attackerContract.totalReceived > singleClaimAmount; lpLocks[token] claimable zeroed only after final re-entry unwinds
IF safe (CEI or internal guard in PORTAL_ROLLER): attackerContract.totalReceived == singleClaimAmount

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: LP fees (BNB or quote token) held by Portal proxy (1361 BNB live balance)
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: Multiple times the single-claim LP fee entitlement per reentrancy depth
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Portal proxy loses BNB/quote token beyond the attacker's legitimate claimable amount
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: If PORTAL_ROLLER reads claimable balance before zeroing it and transfers to caller, each reentrant call reads the same pre-zeroed value and transfers again; attacker receives N * claimable instead of 1 * claimable

setup: Fork BSC at block >= 108382650 using proxy 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0
Decode BeneficiaryClaimed and LaunchedToDEX events to find a token address with non-zero claimable LP fees
Confirm non-zero claimable via getLocks(token) or collectV4Fees preview call
Deploy AttackerContract: stores token address, implements receive() that re-enters Portal.claim(token) once and accumulates totalReceived
Use vm.store to set _tokenBeneficiaries[token] = address(attackerContract) in Portal proxy storage
Resolve PORTAL_ROLLER address from Portal constructor args or immutable storage slot and fetch its verified source from BscScan for static CEI pre-check before running the fork test
expected_assertions: assertEq(attackerContract.totalReceived(), singleClaimAmount, 'safe: CEI or internal guard holds');
// FAIL (totalReceived > singleClaimAmount) => double-claim confirmed, promote to HIGH_CONFIDENCE_CANDIDATE
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If totalReceived == singleClaimAmount the implementation is safe (CEI or internal guard in PORTAL_ROLLER); reject the finding. If no token with non-zero claimable LP fees can be found on the live fork, reject for unmet preconditions.

rejection_reason: Cannot set exact_code_path_exists=true or concrete_fund_or_reward_gain=true because the PORTAL_ROLLER source file is absent from the indexed repository. The entire vulnerability is conditional on PORTAL_ROLLER violating CEI (transferring before zeroing claimable balance). Portal itself has no reentrancy guard (confirmed by grep across all src/**/*.sol), but whether that gap is exploitable depends entirely on PORTAL_ROLLER's internal ordering, which cannot be statically verified without its source. Additionally, live preconditions (token with non-zero claimable LP fees, attacker as registered beneficiary) are unconfirmed from live_context.json. Local fork test with PORTAL_ROLLER source retrieval is required before any promotion.
